Title: User Registration for New Family
Preconditions: User has an Android device with version 8.1 or higher, internet connection, and Firebase account integration enabled.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., "testfamily@gmail.com"), strong password (e.g., "SecurePass123!"), family name (e.g., "Smith"), newborn name (e.g., "John"), newborn birth date (e.g., "15/01/2023").
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app.
2. Navigate to the registration screen.
3. Enter the family email, password, family name, newborn name, and birth date.
4. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message appears. The user is redirected to the login screen or home dashboard.

Title: User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: A registered user account exists in the system.
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app.
2. Navigate to the login screen.
3. Enter the registered email and password.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, and the home dashboard or family profile screen is displayed.

Title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: A registered user account exists in the system.
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app.
2. Navigate to the login screen.
3. Enter the registered email and incorrect password.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Invalid email or password." User remains on the login screen.

Title: Add Newborn Development Milestone
Preconditions: User is logged in to the NewBorn app.
Test Data: Newborn name (e.g., "John"), milestone type (e.g., "Sleep"), milestone description (e.g., "Slept 8 hours"), date (e.g., "16/01/2023"), time (e.g., "20:00").
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app and log in.
2. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
3. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
4. Click the "Add Milestone" button.
5. Enter milestone type, description, date, and time.
6. Click the "Save" button.
Expected Result: Milestone is successfully added to the newborn’s profile, and a confirmation message appears.

Title: View Newborn Development History
Preconditions: User is logged in to the NewBorn app, and at least one milestone exists for the newborn.
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app and log in.
2. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
3. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
4. Click the "View History" button.
Expected Result: A list of all recorded milestones for the newborn is displayed, sorted by date in descending order.

Title: Edit Existing Newborn Milestone
Preconditions: User is logged in to the NewBorn app, and at least one milestone exists for the newborn.
Test Data: Existing milestone (e.g., "Slept 8 hours" on "16/01/2023"), updated description (e.g., "Slept 9 hours").
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app and log in.
2. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
3. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
4. Click the "View History" button.
5. Locate the milestone to edit and click the "Edit" button.
6. Update the milestone description.
7. Click the "Save" button.
Expected Result: Milestone is successfully updated, and a confirmation message appears. The updated milestone is reflected in the history list.

Title: Delete Existing Newborn Milestone
Preconditions: User is logged in to the NewBorn app, and at least one milestone exists for the newborn.
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app and log in.
2. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
3. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
4. Click the "View History" button.
5. Locate the milestone to delete and click the "Delete" button.
6. Confirm deletion in the pop-up dialog.
Expected Result: Milestone is successfully deleted, and a confirmation message appears. The milestone no longer appears in the history list.

Title: Real-Time Data Sync Across Devices for Newborn Milestones
Preconditions: User is logged in on two different Android devices (Device A and Device B) with the same account, and at least one milestone exists for the newborn on Device A.
Test Data: Newborn name (e.g., "John"), milestone added on Device A (e.g., "Fed at 12:00").
Test Steps:
1. On Device A, add a new milestone for the newborn.
2. Wait for Firebase to sync the data (up to 5 seconds).
3. On Device B, navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
4. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
5. Click the "View History" button.
Expected Result: The newly added milestone appears on Device B within 5 seconds, confirming real-time synchronization.

Title: Data Persistence After App Restart
Preconditions: User is logged in to the NewBorn app, and at least one milestone exists for the newborn.
Test Steps:
1. Open the NewBorn app and log in.
2. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
3. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
4. Verify the milestone exists in the history list.
5. Close the app completely (swipe away from recent apps).
6. Reopen the app and log in again.
7. Navigate to the "Child Monitoring" section.
8. Select the newborn (e.g., "John").
9. Click the "View History" button.
Expected Result: The milestone remains visible in the history list after app restart, confirming data persistence.

Title: Successful Account Creation via Google
Preconditions: Application installed, user has a Google account
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. Press the "Sign in with Google" button.
3. Log in with valid Google credentials.
Expected Result: User is automatically redirected to the family creation screen.

Title: Automatic Login for Existing User
Preconditions: User has an existing account
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Press the "Sign in with Google" button.
3. Log in with valid Google credentials.
Expected Result: User is automatically logged in without account creation prompts.

Title: Create New Family with Neonatal Data
Preconditions: User has created an account
Test Steps:
1. Press the "Crea" (Create) button for family creation.
2. Enter neonatal information (name, birth date, gender).
3. Confirm family creation.
Expected Result: Family is created, and user profile is automatically added to the family.

Title: Invite New Member to Existing Family
Preconditions: User has created a family
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to family settings.
2. Select the option to invite new members.
3. Share the family secret key with the new member.
Expected Result: New member can join the family by entering the shared key.

Title: Record Neonatal Sleep Hours
Preconditions: User is a member of a family
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Monitoring section.
2. Select the "Ore di sonno" (Sleep Hours) option.
3. Press the "registra" (record) button.
4. Enter sleep duration (e.g., 14 hours).
Expected Result: Data is recorded and displayed in the graph; data is consultable from the home screen.

Title: Record Neonatal Feeding Time
Preconditions: User is a member of a family
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Monitoring section.
2. Select the "Allattamento" (Feeding) option.
3. Press the "registra" (record) button.
4. Enter feeding duration (e.g., 20 minutes).
Expected Result: Data is recorded and displayed in the graph; data is consultable from the home screen.

Title: Record Neonatal Growth Metrics
Preconditions: User is a member of a family
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Monitoring section.
2. Select the "Crescita bambino" (Child Growth) option.
3. Press the "registra" (record) button.
4. Enter weight (e.g., 4.5 kg) and height (e.g., 50 cm).
Expected Result: Data is recorded and displayed in the graph; data is consultable from the home screen.

Title: Save and Retrieve Monitored Data
Preconditions: User has recorded data in Monitoring section
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Monitoring section.
2. View recorded data snapshots.
3. Navigate to the home screen.
4. Verify data is displayed in graphs.
Expected Result: Previously recorded data is saved and consultable from the home screen.

Title: Create a Family Profile
Preconditions: User is logged in to the application
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Create Family" option in the app.
2. Enter required family details (e.g., family name, members).
3. Confirm creation by selecting "Save" or "Create."
Expected Result: Family profile is successfully created and saved in the system.

Title: Record Sleep Duration
Preconditions: User has created a family profile and added a child
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Sleep Monitoring" section.
2. Select the child for whom sleep duration is to be recorded.
3. Enter the start time of sleep (e.g., 20:00).
4. Enter the end time of sleep (e.g., 06:00).
5. Confirm by selecting "Save" or "Record."
Expected Result: Sleep duration (e.g., 10 hours) is recorded and displayed in the child’s sleep history.

Title: Record Breastfeeding Session with Right Breast Timer
Preconditions: User has created a family profile and added a child
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Feeding Monitoring" section.
2. Select the child for whom feeding data is to be recorded.
3. Start the timer for the right breast.
4. Wait for 15 minutes, then stop the timer.
5. Confirm by selecting "Save" or "Record."
Expected Result: Feeding session duration (15 minutes) for the right breast is recorded and displayed in the child’s feeding history.

Title: Record Breastfeeding Session with Left Breast Timer
Preconditions: User has created a family profile and added a child
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Feeding Monitoring" section.
2. Select the child for whom feeding data is to be recorded.
3. Start the timer for the left breast.
4. Wait for 10 minutes, then stop the timer.
5. Confirm by selecting "Save" or "Record."
Expected Result: Feeding session duration (10 minutes) for the left breast is recorded and displayed in the child’s feeding history.

Title: Record Growth Data
Preconditions: User has created a family profile and added a child
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "Growth Monitoring" section.
2. Select the child for whom growth data is to be recorded.
3. Enter height (e.g., 75 cm), weight (e.g., 8 kg), and date (e.g., 2023-10-01).
4. Confirm by selecting "Save" or "Record."
Expected Result: Growth data is recorded and displayed in the child’s growth history.

Title: Verify Data Synchronization on App Close
Preconditions: User has recorded data (e.g., sleep, feeding, growth) and is online
Test Steps:
1. Record sample data (e.g., sleep duration, feeding session).
2. Close the application.
3. Reopen the application on the same device.
4. Verify recorded data is still present.
5. If applicable, log in to another device and verify the same data is present.
Expected Result: All recorded data is synchronized and accessible across devices after app closure.

Title: Verify App Compatibility with Android 8.1+
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with version 8.1 or higher
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device with version 8.1 or higher.
2. Attempt to open and navigate through the app.
3. Test core functionalities (e.g., family creation, sleep monitoring).
Expected Result: Application installs successfully and all functionalities work without errors on Android 8.1+ devices.

Title: Verify Fluid Navigation in the App
Preconditions: Application is installed and opened
Test Steps:
1. Navigate between different sections (e.g., Sleep Monitoring → Feeding Monitoring → Growth Monitoring).
2. Perform actions in each section (e.g., record sleep, start feeding timer).
3. Observe the time taken for transitions and actions.
Expected Result: Navigation and actions are performed without excessive delays (e.g., <2 seconds per transition/action).

Title: Verify App Functionality Without Internet
Preconditions: Application is installed and opened
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the device.
2. Attempt to record new data (e.g., sleep duration, feeding session).
3. Attempt to navigate to sections requiring cloud data (e.g., synchronized growth history).
Expected Result: App allows recording of data locally but prevents actions requiring cloud synchronization (e.g., syncing data across devices).

Title: Verify Data Privacy for Non-Shared Information
Preconditions: User has recorded sensitive data (e.g., feeding sessions, growth metrics)
Test Steps:
1. Record sensitive data (e.g., feeding duration, growth metrics) without sharing with other users.
2. Attempt to access this data from another user account (if multi-user is supported).
3. Verify data visibility in shared vs. non-shared scenarios.
Expected Result: Sensitive data remains private and is not accessible to other users unless explicitly shared.

Title: Verify automatic data deletion on app uninstallation
Preconditions: App installed on an Android device with Android 8.0 or higher
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android device.
2. Register a new account using a Google account.
3. Enter sample data (e.g., family creation, feeding logs).
4. Uninstall the application from the device.
Expected Result: All user data entered in the application is automatically deleted.

Title: Verify user registration and login with Google account
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. Select the option to log in with Google.
3. Enter valid Google account credentials.
4. Complete the registration process.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and redirected to the main menu.

Title: Verify family creation with newborn data
Preconditions: User is logged in with a valid Google account
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the family creation option.
2. Enter newborn details (e.g., name, birth date).
3. Confirm family creation.
Expected Result: Family is created successfully, and the user is redirected to the home screen.

Title: Verify user invitation to an existing family using a family code
Preconditions: A family already exists with a generated family code
Test Data: Family code shared by an existing member
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the option to join an existing family.
2. Enter the family code provided by an existing member.
3. Confirm the invitation.
Expected Result: New user is added to the existing family, and the family group is updated.

Title: Verify feeding measurement registration
Preconditions: User is part of a family with a newborn
Test Data: Feeding details (timestamp, duration, type of feeding)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the feeding measurement section.
2. Enter feeding details (e.g., timestamp, duration, type of feeding).
3. Save the data.
Expected Result: Feeding data is registered and displayed in the monitoring section.

Title: Verify sleep measurement registration
Preconditions: User is part of a family with a newborn
Test Data: Sleep details (start time, end time, duration)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the sleep measurement section.
2. Enter sleep details (e.g., start time, end time).
3. Save the data.
Expected Result: Sleep data is registered and displayed in the monitoring section.

Title: Verify growth measurement registration
Preconditions: User is part of a family with a newborn
Test Data: Growth details (weight, height, date of measurement)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the growth measurement section.
2. Enter growth details (e.g., weight, height, date).
3. Save the data.
Expected Result: Growth data is registered and displayed in the monitoring section.

Title: Verify user interaction response time
Preconditions: App is installed and running on an Android device
Test Steps:
1. Perform a user interaction (e.g., clicking a button, navigating to a section).
2. Measure the time taken for the interaction to complete.
Expected Result: Interaction completes within 3 seconds.

Title: Verify database response time for device communication
Preconditions: App is connected to the internet
Test Steps:
1. Trigger a database communication (e.g., saving feeding data).
2. Measure the time taken for the database to respond.
Expected Result: Database response time does not exceed 3 seconds.

Title: Verify simultaneous access by multiple users
Preconditions: Multiple users are logged in to the same family
Test Steps:
1. Log in with two different accounts belonging to the same family.
2. Perform simultaneous actions (e.g., updating feeding data).
Expected Result: Both users can access and update data without conflicts.

Title: Verify app compatibility with Android 8.0 or higher
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android devices with OS versions 8.0 (Oreo) and above
Test Steps:
1. Install the app on an Android device with OS version 8.0 (Oreo).
2. Install the app on an Android device with OS version higher than 8.0.
3. Perform basic operations (e.g., login, family creation).
Expected Result: App installs and runs correctly on all tested Android versions.

Title: Verify app memory requirements
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with at least 30MB free memory
Test Steps:
1. Check the device memory before installation.
2. Install the app on the device.
3. Verify the app occupies no more than 30MB of memory.
Expected Result: App installs successfully and occupies ≤30MB of memory.

Title: Verify data persistence until account deletion
Preconditions: User has registered and entered data
Test Steps:
1. Register a new account and enter sample data (e.g., family creation, feeding logs).
2. Log out and log back in.
3. Delete the Google account used for registration.
Expected Result: Data remains accessible until account deletion; after deletion, data is permanently removed.
